payload.learning_objective: Understand vectors as objects with magnitude and direction that can be added and scaled, discovering how vector operations work geometrically through a basis recipe constructor

mini_spec.title: Basis Recipe Constructor
mini_spec.narrative_wrapper: You're given two fixed 'basis' vectors (not perpendicular, not aligned) and a target vector shown in gray. You must construct the target using ONLY whole-number multiples of the two basis vectors—like a recipe using only cups (basis 1) and tablespoons (basis 2). A counter shows your ingredient budget; overspending causes overflow.
mini_spec.manipulated_variable: Two integer spinner controls (up/down buttons or scroll wheels) that set the multiplier coefficients for each basis vector, changing how many copies of each basis vector are combined
mini_spec.primary_visual_feedback: As you increment each spinner, that many copies of the corresponding basis vector appear arranged tip-to-tail along the construction path; the resultant vector (sum) draws in bright color from origin to the final tip position; distance to target updates numerically and as a shrinking gap line
mini_spec.causal_chain_exposed: Each basis vector contribution appears as a separate colored segment in the tip-to-tail chain (e.g., three blue arrows for '3× basis-1', two red arrows for '2× basis-2'); a component breakdown table shows how many units of each basis you're using; the resultant's coordinates appear in terms of the basis vectors, not x/y
mini_spec.trace_strategy: Each time you test a coefficient combination, the resultant vector freezes as a faint ghost with a label showing the coefficients tried (e.g., '2,3'); this creates a growing field of attempt vectors radiating from origin, visually mapping the integer lattice spanned by the two basis vectors
mini_spec.aha_moment: Discovering that you can reach ANY target vector by adjusting just two coefficients—the basis vectors are fundamental building blocks, and the 'numbers' describing a vector are really instructions for combining basis vectors, not inherent properties of space itself
mini_spec.feasibility_sketch: Draggable integer spinners, SVG vector drawing with tip-to-tail path construction, ghost trace layer, and a simple numeric readout table—all standard web primitives; no runtime risk beyond typical canvas repaint.
mini_spec.weakness: The 'overflow' budget mechanic is mentioned in narrative but never specified: unclear what overflow does, when it triggers, or whether it teaches or punishes—this vagueness could undermine the feedback informativeness claim if poorly implemented.

payload.target_misconception: Vectors are just lists of numbers rather than geometric objects with direction and magnitude
payload.section_context: Opening section of a Hilbert space lesson, introducing vectors as the fundamental building blocks before moving to vector spaces, inner products, and completeness
payload.design_brief: This is a lesson on Hilbert spaces for university students with no prior knowledge. The shared domain model is in src/data/model.ts and includes vector operations (addVec2, scaleVec2, linearCombination2, magnitude2). The lesson builds from vectors → vector spaces → inner products → completeness → Hilbert spaces. Maintain a clean, academic tone with soft muted colors (teal #62D0AD for primary, indigo #8E90F5 for secondary).
